title: phlox austromontana subsp. densa
intro: Phlox austromontana subsp. densa is a lesser-known subspecies of the Phlox genus, commonly referred to as a flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of the southwestern United States and Mexico. Traditionally, the plant is used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of Phlox austromontana subsp. densa include its potential to reduce pain and inflammation, as well as its ability to aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Phlox austromontana subsp. densa may be made by infusing the plant in hot water or oil to create teas, tinctures, or salves, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
